faqs.response: Hello! Welcome to Tediwell. How can I help you today?
Our clinic is open Monday to Saturday, 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. We are closed on Sundays and public holidays.
You can book an appointment by calling +1-800-Tediwell or visiting our website at www.Tediwell.com/book.
We have specialists in Cardiology, Orthopedics, Dermatology, Pediatrics, and General Medicine.
We are located at 123 Health Street, Medical District, New York, NY 10001.
We accept most major insurance plans including BlueCross, Aetna, Cigna, and United Health. Please call us to verify your specific plan.
For emergencies, please call 911 immediately. Our emergency helpline is +1-800-MED-HELP (available 24/7).
Our in-house pharmacy is open during clinic hours. Prescriptions can be refilled by contacting your doctor or calling +1-800-MEDICARE.
Consultation fees start at $50 for general medicine. Specialist consultations range from $100–$200. We accept cash, cards, and insurance.
Our diagnostic lab offers blood tests, X-rays, MRI, and more. Reports are available within 24–48 hours. Lab is open 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
We offer COVID-19 testing and vaccinations. Please book an appointment in advance. Walk-ins are accepted based on availability.
You can reach us at +1-800-Tediwell or email us at info@Tediwell.com.
Thank you for contacting MediCare Plus. Stay healthy! Goodbye!
Tedizolid (Tediwell) is an oxazolidinone class antibiotic used to treat acute bacterial skin and skin structure infections caused by gram-positive bacteria.
Tedizolid (Tediwell) works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis through binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit, thereby preventing bacterial growth.
Tedizolid (Tediwell) is indicated for the treatment of acute bacterial skin and skin structure infections (ABSSSI) caused by susceptible gram-positive bacteria.
Tedizolid (Tediwell) is effective against gram-positive pathogens including MRSA (Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus), Streptococcus species, and Enterococcus species.
The usual recommended dose of Tedizolid (Tediwell) is 200 mg once daily for 6 days, as prescribed by a physician.
Yes, Tedizolid (Tediwell) can be taken with or without food.
Key advantages of Tedizolid (Tediwell) include: once daily dosing, shorter treatment duration, and effectiveness against resistant gram-positive bacteria such as MRSA.
Common side effects of Tedizolid (Tediwell) may include: Nausea, Headache, Diarrhea, Vomiting, and Dizziness.
No. Tedizolid (Tediwell) is an antibiotic and is not effective against viral infections such as cold or flu.
Tedizolid (Tediwell) should be used during pregnancy only if prescribed by a doctor after evaluating the benefits and risks.
Patients should inform their doctor about existing medical conditions, current medications, and history of allergy to antibiotics before using Tedizolid (Tediwell).
Tedizolid (Tediwell) offers once-daily dosing, shorter therapy duration, and strong activity against resistant gram-positive bacteria, improving treatment convenience.
